# Proverbs 17

<!-- BEGIN AUTO-INDEX (verse table + nav; auto-generated — safe to regenerate) -->

## Verses (28)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Better a bit of dry bread in peace, than a house full of feasting and violent behaviour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | A servant who does wisely will have rule over a son causing shame, and will have his part in the heritage among brothers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | The heating-pot is for silver and the oven-fire for gold, but the Lord is the tester of hearts. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | A wrongdoer gives attention to evil lips, and a man of deceit gives ear to a damaging tongue. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Whoever makes sport of the poor puts shame on his Maker; and he who is glad because of trouble will not go free from punishment. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | Children's children are the crown of old men, and the glory of children is their fathers. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | Fair words are not to be looked for from a foolish man, much less are false lips in a ruler. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | An offering of money is like a stone of great price in the eyes of him who has it: wherever he goes, he does well. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | He who keeps a sin covered is looking for love; but he who keeps on talking of a thing makes division between friends. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | A word of protest goes deeper into one who has sense than a hundred blows into a foolish man. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | An uncontrolled man is only looking for trouble, so a cruel servant will be sent against him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | It is better to come face to face with a bear whose young ones have been taken away than with a foolish man acting foolishly. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | If anyone gives back evil for good, evil will never go away from his house. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | The start of fighting is like the letting out of water: so give up before it comes to blows. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | He who gives a decision for the evil-doer and he who gives a decision against the upright, are equally disgusting to the Lord. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | How will money in the hand of the foolish get him wisdom, seeing that he has no sense? | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | A friend is loving at all times, and becomes a brother in times of trouble. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | A man without sense gives his hand in an agreement, and makes himself responsible before his neighbour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | The lover of fighting is a lover of sin: he who makes high his doorway is looking for destruction. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | Nothing good comes to him whose heart is fixed on evil purposes: and he who has an evil tongue will come to trouble. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | He who has an unwise son gets sorrow for himself, and the father of a foolish son has no joy. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | A glad heart makes a healthy body, but a crushed spirit makes the bones dry. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | A sinner takes an offering out of his robe, to get a decision for himself in a cause. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | Wisdom is before the face of him who has sense; but the eyes of the foolish are on the ends of the earth. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | A foolish son is a grief to his father, and bitter pain to her who gave him birth. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | To give punishment to the upright is not good, or to give blows to the noble for their righteousness. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | He who has knowledge says little: and he who has a calm spirit is a man of good sense. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | Even the foolish man, when he keeps quiet, is taken to be wise: when his lips are shut he is credited with good sense. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
